expectations.text: Decomposes into 4 work units with no dependencies between them
expectations.evidence: Step 8 Phase 2 produces 4 work units: health-endpoint, list-users-endpoint, create-user-endpoint, get-user-endpoint. All have dependsOn=[] (empty array). Each is independent with its own test and impl files.

expectations.text: Computes a single parallel batch containing all 4 units
expectations.evidence: Step 10: 'All 4 units have dependsOn: []. ALL are immediately ready. Ready queue: [health-endpoint, list-users-endpoint, create-user-endpoint, get-user-endpoint]. maxParallelPairs = 4. Pop all 4 — dispatch all 4 concurrently. Single flush fills all 4 slots.'

expectations.text: Presents the execution plan showing all units run concurrently in Batch 1
expectations.evidence: User Confirmation in Step 8: 'Execution plan (eager dispatch, max 4 concurrent): Ready immediately: health-endpoint, list-users-endpoint, create-user-endpoint, get-user-endpoint (all parallel).' All 4 shown as ready immediately in a single concurrent batch.

expectations.text: Dispatches multiple Test Writers simultaneously (up to maxParallelPairs config, default 4)
expectations.evidence: Step 10 heading: 'ALL 4 UNITS RUN IN PARALLEL (Slots 1-4).' Execution timeline shows all 4 pipelines starting at t0: 'Slot 1: [health-endpoint: TW -> RED -> CW -> GREEN -> SC -> AR -> CQ]. Slot 2: [list-users...]. Slot 3: [create-user...]. Slot 4: [get-user...].' All 4 Test Writers dispatched simultaneously.

expectations.text: Each unit independently goes through the full RED->GREEN->review cycle
expectations.evidence: Each of the 4 units has its own complete pipeline documented: health-endpoint (Steps 4a-4g), list-users-endpoint (4a-4g), create-user-endpoint (4a-4g), get-user-endpoint (4a-4g). Each includes Test Writer, RED, Code Writer, GREEN, Spec Compliance, Adversarial, and Code Quality reviews.

expectations.text: Applies the three-stage review pipeline: spec compliance -> adversarial -> code quality
expectations.evidence: For each unit, the review order is: Step 4e (Spec Compliance) -> Step 4f (Adversarial) -> Step 4g (Code Quality). This strict ordering is maintained across all 4 units. health-endpoint shows: 'Spec Compliance Review -> Adversarial Review -> Code Quality Review.' Same for other units.

expectations.text: Runs a final integration test after all units complete
expectations.evidence: Step 11 Phase 5: 'Run FULL test suite: npx vitest run. All 4 test files pass. Pristine output: no warnings, no skipped, no pending. Holistic review: 4 independent endpoint modules. Check for naming conflicts. Check for shared utility patterns.' Integration review performed after all units complete.

claims: All 4 independent work units dispatched simultaneously in a single flush of the ready queue
maxParallelPairs=4 default exactly matches the unit count, enabling maximum concurrency
Each unit runs its complete TDD pipeline independently: TW -> RED -> CW -> GREEN -> SC -> AR -> CQ
Three-stage review ordering enforced per unit: spec compliance first, then adversarial, then code quality
Total wall-clock time equals the duration of the slowest single pipeline (optimal for independent work)
Final integration review checks for cross-unit concerns (naming conflicts, shared patterns)

suggestions: Consider testing with 5+ units to exercise the maxParallelPairs=4 cap (would need 2 dispatch waves even with no dependencies).

The trace notes the design gate is 'borderline' but skipped. A variant with less explicit independence ('implement a REST API with 4 endpoints') without the word 'independent' could test whether the skill still correctly decomposes.
All 4 units are Mechanical complexity with sonnet/high. A variant with mixed complexity independent units would test per-unit model assignment in the parallel case.
eval_feedback.overall: The with_skill run demonstrates maximum concurrency for fully independent work units. All 7 assertions pass. The trace clearly shows all 4 units dispatched simultaneously, each running its complete TDD pipeline in parallel, with the three-stage review enforced per unit. This is the ideal case for eager dispatch: no dependencies, maxParallelPairs matches unit count, single-flush execution.
